title: 続きを進めて。kernelで進めて。 コスト前提を設計に入れます。契約済みの Claude / GPT / GLM を通常ループの主 軸にし、Copilot を低コスト補助、Gemini と xAI は overflow と tie-break に 限定する方針で /kernel 契約を更新します。 • Updated Plan └ Adding a cost-aware redesign layer to /kernel based on the user's subscription and metered-provider constraints. I will encode provi...
summary_text: 1. `/kernel` bootstrap must prove `6+` concurrently active lanes before substantive work, with explicit lane tags (`phase/family/provider_hint`) and no counting of planned/failed/pending lanes. 2. Baseline council continuity must hold across planning, simulation, critique, repair, replan, implementation, and QA: `Codex + Claude + GLM` when available; otherwise fail closed unless runtime-proven unavailability triggers valid fallback quorum. 3. Valid fallback quorum must be `Codex + 2 non-Codex lanes`; `Codex`-only or `Codex + 1 non-Codex` is always `BLOCKED` and treated as orchestration policy violation. 4. Cost policy must be executable in scheduler/runtime: fixed-cost lanes first (`Codex/GPT`, `Claude`, `GLM`), `Copilot` as low-cost continuity, and `Gemini/xAI` only when `metered_reason ∈ {overflow, tie-break}`. 5. `/kernel -> /vote` handoff must preserve `kernel_handoff_mode` and cost/fallback snapshot (`cost_*`, `metered_reason`, `fallback_used`, `missing_lane`, `fallback_provider`, `fallback_reason`) and `/vote` must continue execution without re-running redesign rounds. 6. Readiness gates/tests must validate runtime invariants (lane floor, trio/fallback quorum, fail-close behavior, metered-lane restriction, handoff field propagation), not only prompt text presence. 7. Fail-close behavior must be deterministic: if lane minimum/quorum/bridge-authoritative provider probe conditions are unmet, execution stops with machine-readable `BLOCKED` output including requested action, active lanes, missing lanes, attempted probes, and exact error reason. 8. Final runtime evidence must show visible lane model and adjudication lineage (which lanes actually executed, which fallback lane replaced whom, and why), sufficient to audit contract compliance end-to-end.
